title: [SMOKE TEST] Pre-registration falsified: 1 Mb biophysics-vs-recombination is NOT just an isochore rediscovery — observed signals exceed all pre-registered null thresholds

subject.display: Pre-registration falsified: 1 Mb biophysics-vs-recombination is NOT just an isochore rediscovery — observed signals exceed all pre-registered null thresholds
subject.prose: Migrated from v1.1 as a literal subject because the claim was marked withdrawn/falsified at migration time. Re-author with a typed subject when resuming.

structured.note: literal wrap chosen due to withdrawn/falsified status

context.free_form: migrated from v1.1 without domain-specific context

source.note: Pre-registered hypothesis-collapse thresholds from DESIGN.md /neg observation #1: the team expected 1 Mb to only confirm Bernardi isochores, with partial r < 0.05 for non-GC features and ΔAUROC < 0.05 for the RF model.
Headline statistics from RESULT.md A1, A2, A6 — the analyses that bear on the isochore-rediscovery prediction.

statistics.name: a1_max_abs_partial_r_after_gc_observed
statistics.note: Maximum |partial r| across the 64 biophysical features after GC residualization (NCO x cpg_density). Pre-registered ceiling under isochore-rediscovery null was 0.05.

statistics.name: a1_pre_registered_max_partial_r_under_null
statistics.note: From DESIGN.md H1: 'partial r < 0.05 — confirmatory of GC dominance at this scale.'

statistics.name: a6_delta_auroc_m2_minus_m0_observed
statistics.note: From the canonical exp17 fixture: M2 (biophys-minus-GC) AUROC 0.827 vs M0 (GC-only) 0.700.

statistics.name: a6_pre_registered_max_delta_auroc_under_null
statistics.note: From DESIGN.md Pass/Fail: 'STRONG positive: ΔAUROC > 0.05 OR translocation enrichment > 2x.' Below 0.05 was implicitly the null.

inference.justification: Two conjuncts: observed values must exceed BOTH pre-registered ceilings — A1 partial r and A6 ΔAUROC. The conjunction structurally licenses the supersession of the isochore-rediscovery hypothesis. Note: the conclusion outcome is 'negative' because what is being negated is the pre-registered HYPOTHESIS, not the observed result. The observed result is itself strongly positive (and lives in the canonical exp17 fixture); this claim is the meta-observation that the 1 Mb isochore-rediscovery null was wrong.
inference.failure_mode: If either observed value fell at or below its pre-registered ceiling, the isochore-rediscovery null would have held and the canonical exp17 result would need a different framing.

conclusion.assertion: The pre-registered hypothesis (DESIGN.md /neg #1: at 1 Mb resolution, biophysics-recombination is just isochore rediscovery; partial r < 0.05; ΔAUROC < 0.05) is FALSIFIED. Observed: max |partial r| = 0.37 (7.4-fold above ceiling), ΔAUROC(M2-M0) = +0.128 (2.6-fold above ceiling). The 1 Mb scale carries massive non-isochore signal; sub-1-Mb extensions are bonus, not required.

scope.scale_note: Meta-claim about a pre-registered null. The claim being falsified is an EXPECTATION; the observed result lives in the canonical exp17 fixture (depended on).

confidence.note: Confidence reflects the magnitude of the observation-vs-pre-registration gap, not a statistical test.

external_assumptions.statement: The DESIGN.md pre-registration ceilings (partial r < 0.05, ΔAUROC < 0.05) are good-faith null expectations, not strawmen. /neg observation #1 documented them before the analyses were run.

This claim supersedes the pre-registered isochore-rediscovery hypothesis. v1.1 schema has no first-class supersedes/superseded_by field; the supersession relationship is documented in recombination hotspots/claims/README.md (claim 10) until v1.2.
